summary: The candidate's proposed DKD, AD-risk, IPF, and coverage-assessment updates map consistently to the senolytics track, D+Q intervention class, human evidence tiers, endpoint categories, and cellular-senescence/chronic-inflammation hallmark scope. Taxonomy limitations are explicitly carried as maturity and coverage gaps rather than being collapsed into broader efficacy claims.

findings.claim_or_issue: The proposed study, outcome, result, finding, and coverage-assessment records consistently place dasatinib plus quercetin under intervention_id dasatinib-quercetin and track_id senolytics.
findings.why_it_matters: Consistent intervention and track mapping keeps the repair within the senolytics evidence track instead of creating parallel D+Q or indication-specific buckets.
findings.recommended_action: Retain the current intervention and track mappings.

findings.claim_or_issue: The DKD biomarker records, AD-risk cognition/mobility records, and IPF dosing-completion records all retain cellular_senescence and chronic_inflammation hallmark scope while separating biomarker, functional, safety, and other endpoint categories.
findings.why_it_matters: This separation prevents biomarker or feasibility findings from being treated as generalized clinical efficacy while preserving the mechanistic senescence/SASP rationale.
findings.recommended_action: Retain the endpoint-category and hallmark mappings as written.

findings.claim_or_issue: The coverage assessment and proposed records label DKD, IPF, and AD-risk updates as abstract_extracted or triage_summary where appropriate and keep full-text, source-snapshot, and safety-table work in known gaps.
findings.why_it_matters: Encoding maturity and remaining gaps separately from track or hallmark taxonomy reduces the risk of overstating the evidence tier or review readiness.
findings.recommended_action: Keep the maturity_status values and coverage gap notes attached until source-snapshot-backed extraction and table reconciliation are complete.
